steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined sentence. Determine what the sentence does in relation to the rest of the passage — does it introduce, contrast, extend, or evaluate?

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It asserts that the hyperpop sound hasn't changed, even though new artists have adopted it.: Wrong direction. The sentence says Charli XCX contributed to the sound by incorporating pop melodies — that is a change, not stasis. ✗
  • Choice B — It criticizes a later hyperpop artist for being too similar to an earlier hyperpop artist.: No criticism anywhere in the sentence — it simply states a contribution. ✗
  • Choice C — It praises a particular hyperpop artist for consistently evolving through the years.: The sentence mentions one contribution, not a track record of consistent evolution, and there is no evaluative praise. ✗
  • Choice D — It identifies an artist who has contributed a new stylistic element to the hyperpop sound.: The sentence names Charli XCX and states she added pop melodies — a new stylistic element — to the existing hyperpop sound. That is exactly what D says. ✓
D is correct.
